system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:

vertices

    ( 0.3048  0.09144  -0.005)  // 9 (same as vertex 2)
    ( 0.3048  0.1524   -0.005)  // 10 (same as vertex 4)
    (-0.15242  0.1524   -0.005)  // 11 (same as vertex 7)

);

blocks
(

    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(

        );

);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}

thermoType
{

    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;

}

mixture
{
    specie
    {

    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1.0;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (10 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (10 0 0);
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (10 0 0);
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {

    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;

}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(0 3 2 1) 4(8 9 10 11) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/experiment-finetuned-allrun_commands/single_knockout/knockout_allrun_commands/runs/wedge/2/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 39.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis>## Error Analysis

### Error 1: `log.blockMesh` â€” Inward-pointing face normals

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(0 3 2 1) 4(8 9 10 11)
```

This means the **bottom face** of each hexahedral block has its normal pointing inward (into the block), rather than outward (away from the block). Looking at the vertices:

- **Block 0**: vertices `(0,1,2,3)` form the bottom face. Vertex 0 = (-0.15242, 0, -0.005), vertex 1 = (0.3048, 0, -0.005), vertex 2 = (0.3048, 0.09144, -0.005), vertex 3 = (-0.15242, 0.09144, -0.005).
  - The right-hand rule applied to `(0â†’1â†’2â†’3)` gives a normal pointing **downward** (negative z-direction), into the block.
  - For a proper hex block, the bottom face should have vertices ordered so the normal points **upward** (positive z-direction).

- **Block 1**: vertices `(8,9,10,11)` form the bottom face. Vertices 8 and 9 are duplicates of 3 and 2 respectively, and 10 and 11 duplicate 4 and 7. Same issue.

**Root Cause:** The vertex ordering for blocks 0 and 1 places the bottom face vertices in clockwise order when viewed from outside the block, resulting in an inward-pointing normal.

**Fix:** Swap two adjacent vertices in each block definition to reverse the winding direction of the bottom face. Change:
- Block 0: `hex (0 1 2 3 ...)` â†’ `hex (0 3 2 1 ...)`
- Block 1: `hex (8 9 10 11 ...)` â†’ `hex (8 11 10 9 ...)`

Alternatively, reorder the entire block definitions consistently so that the first four vertices define the bottom face with upward-pointing normals.

---

### Error 2: `log.rhoCentralFoam` â€” Missing mesh

After fixing the mesh generation error, `blockMesh` must succeed before running the solver. Once the mesh is properly generated, the solver should proceed.

---

## Additional Issues Identified

While the primary fix addresses the immediate crash, there are several secondary mismatches between the current setup and the **wedge15Ma5 reference case**:

1. **Boundary Condition Type Mismatch**:
   - Current: `bottom` uses `symmetryPlane`
   - Reference: Should use `empty` (as per wedge15Ma5)
   - **Recommendation**: Change `bottom` from `type symmetryPlane;` to `type empty;` since this is a 2D wedge problem modeled as a 3D extruded domain with periodic boundaries in z.

2. **Inlet Boundary Conditions**:
   - Current: `inlet` uses `fixedValue` for all fields (`U`, `T`, `p`)
   - Reference: Uses `totalPressure` for `p` (waveTransmissive + totalPressure scheme)
   - **Recommendation**: Change `inlet` pressure BC from `fixedValue` to `waveTransmissive` with appropriate `totalPressure` settings to avoid reflections.

3. **Outlet Boundary Conditions**:
   - Current: `outlet` uses `inletOutlet` for `U` and `zeroGradient` for `T`
   - Reference: Typically uses `totalPressure` for `p` and `inletOutlet`/`zeroGradient` appropriately for others
   - **Recommendation**: Ensure consistency with reference case's outlet BCs.

4. **Thermodynamic Properties**:
   - The `physicalProperties` file sets `energy sensibleInternalEnergy`, which is correct for `rhoCentralFoam`. However, verify that the `hePsiThermo` setup matches exactly what the reference case uses (especially regarding `hConst` vs `h` formulation).

5. **Numerical Schemes**:
   - The `fvSchemes` appears consistent with the reference case (Kurganov flux scheme, vanLeer reconstructions). No changes needed here once the mesh is fixed.

---

## Summary of Actions

1. **Primary Fix**: Reorder vertices in blocks 0 and 1 of `blockMeshDict` so that the bottom face normals point outward (use `hex (0 3 2 1 ...)` and `hex (8 11 10 9 ...)`).
2. **Secondary Fixes**: Update boundary condition types and values to better match the wedge15Ma5 reference case, particularly changing `bottom` from `symmetryPlane` to `empty` and adjusting inlet/outlet BCs accordingly.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change block 0 vertex ordering from hex (0 1 2 3 ...) to hex (0 3 2 1 ...) so bottom face winds counterclockwise when viewed from above; Change block 1 vertex ordering from hex (8 9 10 11 ...) to hex (8 11 10 9 ...) so bottom face winds counterclockwise when viewed from above'}, {'file': '0/U', 'changes': 'Change bottom boundary type from symmetryPlane to empty; Change top boundary type from inletOutlet to zeroGradient; Remove inletValue entry from top boundary sub-dictionary'}, {'file': '0/T', 'changes': 'Remove the top boundary sub-dictionary entirely since it will inherit from zeroGradient via the empty boundary type; Keep bottom as zeroGradient'}, {'file': '0/p', 'changes': 'Keep bottom as symmetryPlane; Keep frontAndBack as empty'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
